figures.caption: Table 2. Correlations between baseline predictive accuracy of gait parameters and metabolic cost trend prediction accuracy across 8 participants.

Fig 4. Correlation coefficients and NRMSE of assisted conditions for kinematics (a) and muscle activation (b) between experimental and simulated conditions for the average of all participants. The NRMSE values equal to or above 1 have the same color (white).

Fig 6. The left figure shows the perceived design space from the best optimization run for P.3, with the green diamond marking the identified optimal design. The right figure displays the experimentally measured metabolic costs, with the green diamond indicating the best tested condition.

Fig 2. Correlation coefficients and NRMSE of comparing baseline (NS) kinematics (a) and muscle activation (b) between experimental and simulated movement. P.1 to P.8 refer to the 8 participants. The NRMSE values equal to or above 1 have the same color (white).

Fig 1. The Design Optimization Platform of (28) and the integrated schematic overview of the BATEX device and its model added to the neuromuscular model of the user. The HAM and RF springs are indicated with green and red colors, respectively. τh and τk are the resulting hip and knee torques, directly added to the model’s joints. rh, rk, θh, and θk represent the lever arms of the springs and the hip and knee joint angles. The design space of the two spring stiffness values (device’s design parameters) are within the range of 0-1200 N.m−1.

Fig 5. The left figure shows the perceived design space from the best optimization run for P.5, with the green diamond marking the identified optimal design. The right figure displays the experimentally measured metabolic costs, with the green diamond indicating the best tested condition.
Table 4. Results of the personalized BATEX design optimization.

Fig 3. Participant averaged normalized metabolic cost for the experimentally tested conditions across the design space (kRF and kHAM ). White circles indicate the experimentally tested spring stiffness configurations, and the green diamonds mark the best experimental and simulation conditions. The contour lines highlight the trends of changes.
